questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words. Raman had always been attracted to the scientific study of sound and decided to take up a course in sound engineering to pursue his dream career
questions.explanation: A) Acoustics(noun) – the scientific study of sound. ध्वनिविज्ञान

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. When we believe that our mind is thinking ______ thoughts at the same time, what actually is happening is that __________ thoughts are __________ in such quick ___________ so as to seem simultaneous
questions.explanation: C) multiple, myriad, alternating, successionFirst Blank: 'multiple': ‘Multiple’ का अर्थ है कई – sentence में कहा गया है कि हम सोचते हैं हमारा दिमाग एक ही समय में कई विचार सोच रहा है, इसलिए 'multiple' सही है।जबकि 'homogenous' (एक जैसे), 'challenging' (चुनौतीपूर्ण), और 'uniform' (एकरूप) इस context में विचारों की संख्या को नहीं दर्शाते। • ‘Multiple’ means many – the sentence suggests we think the mind is handling many thoughts at once, so it fits.• 'Homogenous' means same in kind, 'Challenging' means difficult, 'Uniform' means consistent – none imply variety in thought. Second Blank: 'myriad': ‘Myriad’ का अर्थ है असंख्य या अनगिनत – यह दर्शाता है कि विचार बहुत सारी संख्या में होते हैं।जबकि 'measurable' (मापने योग्य), 'limited' (सीमित), 'countable' (गिनने योग्य) विचारों की अनगिनत प्रकृति को नहीं दर्शाते। • ‘Myriad’ expresses countless – fitting for the quick flow of thoughts.• Other options restrict quantity, which breaks the intended meaning. Third Blank: 'alternating': ‘Alternating’ का अर्थ है एक के बाद एक बदलते रहना – यहाँ बताया गया है कि विचार जल्दी-जल्दी एक के बाद एक आ रहे हैं।जबकि 'unchanging' (जो नहीं बदलता), 'fixed' (स्थिर), और 'altering' (बदलता हुआ) क्रम के बदलाव को स्पष्ट नहीं करते। • ‘Alternating’ fits as it means shifting one after the other.• Other words like 'unchanging' or 'fixed' go against the dynamic nature of thoughts. Fourth Blank: 'succession': ‘Succession’ का अर्थ है लगातार एक के बाद एक होना – sentence में विचारों के जल्दी-जल्दी आने का उल्लेख है।'Velocity' (गति), 'interruption' (विघटन), और 'ramification' (परिणाम/शाखा) इस अर्थ में नहीं आते। • ‘Succession’ works because it means a sequence – thoughts appear one after another.• Other options focus on speed, breaks, or outcomes, which don’t fit.

questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. Climbing Mount Everest is an arduous task that requires exceptional physical strength and endurance.
questions.explanation: B) Arduous(adjective) – Difficult, Challenging, Tough, Strenuous, Laborious कठिन Antonym: Effortless(adjective) – Requiring no physical or mental exertion, easy. आसान Combat(verb) – Fight, battle, counter, oppose लड़ना Hoard(verb) – Accumulate, stockpile, collect, store इकट्ठा करना Lanky(adjective) – Tall and thin, often in a way that suggests they move awkwardly. लंबा और दुबला

questions.question: Select the most appropriate word for the given group of words. To have a very high opinion of oneself.
questions.explanation: D) Conceit(noun) – To have an excessively high opinion of oneself. अहंकार

questions.question: In the given sentence, identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. Airlines have increased fares because according to the new rules only limiting passengers are allowed
questions.explanation: A) limiting'के बदले 'limited' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि 'only' के बाद आने वाला शब्द Adjective होना चाहिए, न कि Verb का 'ing' form; जैसे—Only qualified candidates can apply. limited' will be used instead of 'limiting' because the word following 'only' should be an Adjective, not the 'ing' form of a Verb; Like—Only qualified candidates can apply.

questions.question: Select the best express synonym of the given underlined word. As we discussed future projects, we tried to envisage the outcomes of our new strategies.
questions.explanation: D) Envisage(verb) – Imagine, foresee, anticipate, conceive, predict परिकल्पनाकरना Synonym: Foresee(verb) – Be aware of beforehand; predict, envisage. पूर्वानुमान करना Voluminous(adjective) – Of great size, extent, or amount, bulky, extensive. विशाल Miracle(noun) – A surprising and welcome event that is not explicable by natural or scientific laws and is considered to be divine. चमत्कार Commodious(adjective) – Spacious and convenient, capacious, roomy. बड़ा

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the most suitable word. Still flaccid and ______ from lack of blood, Ed ambled slowly in front of the doors.
questions.explanation: C) lethargic'का use होगा क्योंकि "lethargic" का अर्थ होता है थकान या ऊर्जा की कमी के कारण धीमा या निष्क्रिय होना। sentence में बताया गया है कि Ed खून की कमी के कारण धीरे-धीरे चल रहे हैं, जो उनकी थकावट या निष्क्रियता को दर्शाता है, इसलिए 'lethargic' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Liberal' का अर्थ है उदार या खुले विचारों वाला, 'Levity' का अर्थ है हल्कापन या मजाकिया व्यवहार, और 'Levy' का अर्थ है कर लगाना या वसूलना, जो इस context में उपयुक्त नहीं है। Lethargic'should be used because it means affected by lethargy; sluggish and apathetic. The sentence indicates that Ed is moving slowly in front of the doors due to a lack of blood, suggesting his fatigue or sluggishness, making 'lethargic' appropriate here. Whereas, 'Liberal' means open to new behavior or opinions, 'Levity' means lightness of manner or humor, and 'Levy' means imposing a tax or fine, which are not suitable in this context.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. A lean patch
options: A part of hair that is thin
Cloth that is beginning to tear
questions.explanation: C) A lean patch(idiom) – A period of poor performance खराब प्रदर्शन की अवधि

questions.question: Select the best express synonym of the given underlined word. His sudden outburst of laughter at the quiet dinner party startled everyone, turning heads as the room fell silent.
questions.explanation: B) Outburst(noun) – Explosion, eruption, sudden reaction, emotional burst, flare-up तीव्र प्रतिक्रिया Synonym:Explosion(noun) – A sudden and violent blowing apart, a burst of anger or emotion. विस्फोट React(verb) – To act in response to something, to respond in a particular way. प्रतिक्रिया करना Clap(verb) – To applaud or show approval by clapping hands together. ताली बजाना Facilitate(verb) – To make something possible or easier, to assist or aid. सहायक होना

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required.’ These days I ate lots of fruits and green vegetables to boost my immunity.
questions.explanation: C) 'ate'के बदले 'eat' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि वाक्य Present Tense में है और 'These days' से यह इंगित होता है कि क्रिया वर्तमान समय में निरंतर चल रही है। अत: Simple Present Tense में Verb का प्रयोग होगा; जैसे— I eat lots of fruits and green vegetables to boost my immunity. 'eat' will be used instead of 'ate' because the sentence is in the Present Tense, and 'These days' indicates that the action is ongoing in the present time. Hence, the Verb will be in Simple Present Tense; Like— I eat lots of fruits and green vegetables to boost my immunity.

questions.question: Select the appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Last resort
questions.explanation: D)Last resort(idiom) – Last course of action अंतिम उपाय

questions.question: Choose the word that can substitute the given group of words. Compulsory enlistment for military service
questions.explanation: B) Conscription(noun) – Compulsory enlistment for military service सैन्य सेवा के लिए अनिवार्य भर्ती

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required’. I always stays at a hotel which is near my work place.
questions.explanation: D) 'always stay'का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि subject 'I' है, जिसके साथ 'stays' का प्रयोग नहीं होता। Present Simple Tense में, पहले person singular के साथ 'stay' का ही प्रयोग होता है; जैसे— I always stay at a hotel which is near my workplace always stay'will be used instead of 'stays' because the subject is 'I', with which 'stays' is not used. In Present Simple Tense, 'stay' is used with the first person singular; Like— I always stay at a hotel which is near my workplace

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. It is essential for the marketer to study the behaviour of the consumers in order to make better strategic marketing decisions. B. The study of consumer behaviour is equally important for the non-profit organisations such as governmental agencies, hospitals, NGOs, charitable organisations. C. Thus, by studying the consumer behaviour before and during purchase helps in production scheduling, designing, pricing, positioning, segmentation, advertising and other promotional activities. D. If the marketer has complete knowledge about the consumer’s likings or disliking, then he can predict the response of the potential customers towards his offerings
questions.explanation: C) ADCB A starts the paragraph by introducing the subject—the marketer—and the main idea: the importance of studying consumer behavior for strategic decisions. D follows A because it logically extends the idea by explaining what happens if the marketer has knowledge about consumer preferences. The pronoun "he" refers back to "the marketer" in A, maintaining subject continuity. C comes after D as it summarizes the outcome of studying consumer behavior (mentioned in A and D) by listing specific benefits like pricing, advertising, etc. The transition word "Thus" signals a conclusion based on the previous points. B concludes the paragraph by expanding the scope of the discussion to non-profit organizations, showing the broader relevance of consumer behavior studies.In Hindi A paragraph की शुरुआत करता है क्योंकि यह subject (the marketer) और main idea (consumer behaviour study) को introduce करता है। D, A के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह बताता है कि अगर marketer को consumer की पसंद का पूरा ज्ञान है (complete knowledge), तो वह उनकी प्रतिक्रिया predict कर सकता है। यह A के विचार को आगे बढ़ाता है। C, D के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह "Thus" (इस प्रकार) से शुरू होकर परिणाम बताता है—consumer behaviour study से production, pricing, advertising आदि में मदद मिलती है। B अंत में आता है क्योंकि यह scope बढ़ाता है, यह बताता है कि consumer behaviour study NGOs, hospitals आदि के लिए भी उतनी ही महत्वपूर्ण है।

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Sloppy
questions.explanation: B)Sloppy(adjective) – (used about clothes) not tight and without much shape (कपड़े) तंग और बेढंगे Antonym: Dashing(adjective) – Stylish, lively, attractive, and full of confidence. उम्दा/ स्टाइलिश तरीके से आकर्षक Untidy(adjective) – Not neat or orderly; messy, disorganized. अस्त-व्यस्त Rough(adjective) – Not smooth; uneven or irregular in surface, texture, or manner. मोटा Humid(adjective) – Having a high amount of moisture or vapor in the air. आर्द्र

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. They can help you to decide the right products for you. B. Advertisements can be extremely useful if they are honest. C. Teenagers are especially vulnerable to such advertisements. D. However, some advertisements may be harmful as they try to befool you.
questions.explanation: B) BADC B starts the sentence with the subject "Advertisements", introducing the main topic and their positive aspect (being useful if honest). This sets the context for the paragraph. A follows B as it logically connects with the pronoun "They" (referring to "Advertisements" from B) and explains how they help in decision-making. This maintains subject continuity and adds supporting details. D comes next as it introduces a contrast ("However") to the previous idea, shifting the focus to harmful advertisements that try to deceive. This creates a balanced argument. C concludes the paragraph by specifying a vulnerable group ("Teenagers") affected by such harmful ads, completing the flow from general to specific. In Hindi:B sentence की शुरुआत करता है, क्योंकि यह subject "Advertisements" को introduce करता है और उनके positive पहलू (ईमानदार होने पर उपयोगी) को बताता है। यह paragraph का context set करता है। A, B के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह pronoun "They" (B के "Advertisements" को refer करता है) का उपयोग करके बताता है कि वे कैसे निर्णय लेने में मदद करते हैं। यह subject की continuity बनाए रखता है। D, A के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह contrast ("However") introduce करता है और harmful advertisements के बारे में बताता है, जो पिछले विचार से विपरीत है। C paragraph को conclude करता है, यह बताता है कि "Teenagers" (एक vulnerable group) ऐसे harmful ads से प्रभावित होते हैं, जिससे general से specific की ओर flow पूरा होता है।

questions.question: Fill in the blank with an appropriate option. Until you confess your crime, the abdomen would ______ to grow and grow until you could not breathe
questions.explanation: C) 'Continue'का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि "continue" का अर्थ है किसी कार्य या स्थिति को निरंतर जारी रखना। वाक्य में बताया गया है कि जब तक आप अपराध स्वीकार नहीं करते, पेट बढ़ता रहेगा। यहाँ 'continue' इस संदर्भ में सही शब्द है क्योंकि यह बढ़ते रहने की अवधारणा को दर्शाता है। Continue'should be used because it means to keep going or persist in an activity or state. The sentence describes a scenario where the abdomen would keep growing as long as the crime is not confessed. Here, 'continue' is appropriate as it conveys the idea of ongoing growth.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word
questions.explanation: B)the incorrectly spelled word is 'television', The incorrectly spelled word is'talevision'. दूरदर्शन

questions.question: Select the option that rectifies the incorrectly spelt words in the given sentence. The table was grased with a lovelly showpiece
options: The table was grased with a lovvely showpiece.
The table was graced with a lovaly showpiece.
The table was graced with a lovely showpiece.
The table was graced with a lovelly showpiece.
questions.explanation: C)The table was graced with a lovely showpiece.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Only five minutes / are allowed / for each speaker / during the function
questions.explanation: A)'are allowed'के बदले 'is allowed' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि 'Only five minutes' एक Singular Noun Phrase है और इसके लिए Singular Verb का प्रयोग किया जाना चाहिए; जैसे— Only one minute is allowed for each task. 'is allowed' will be used instead of 'are allowed' because 'Only five minutes' is a Singular Noun Phrase and should be followed by a Singular Verb; Like— Only one minute is allowed for each task.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. It’s 1. _________to have a conversation about health and wellness without talking about mental health. In the United States, nearly one in five adults has a mental illness or mental health condition. That’s nearly 52 million people. However, mental health isn’t just about having or not having a mental illness. Our wellbeing is 2. _________ by all aspects of ourlives and that, in turn, affects how we experience life, work and time with our loved ones.Now, the conversation is more 3. _________than ever. In 2021, the prevalent feeling of theyear was languishing. Research found that more than 55% of workers weren’t mentally ill, butthey weren’t thriving either. As the pandemic has brought physical health at the centre stagein an unprecedented way, it’s time 4. _________ look at the other major components of wellbeing. The conversation around mental health awareness has never been as public, asprevalent, or as important. After all, building 5. _________ is a critical ingredient to learninghow to care for your mental health. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: C)'Impossible'का use यहाँ सही होगा क्योंकि "impossible" का अर्थ होता है कुछ ऐसा जो संभव नहीं है। वाक्य में कहा गया है कि "स्वास्थ्य और कल्याण के बारे में मानसिक स्वास्थ्य की चर्चा के बिना बातचीत करना संभव नहीं है।" यह वाक्य उस तथ्य को इंगित करता है कि स्वास्थ्य और कल्याण की चर्चा में मानसिक स्वास्थ्य को शामिल करना अत्यंत आवश्यक है। जबकि 'Beneficial' का अर्थ है लाभदायक, 'Advisable' का अर्थ है सलाह देने योग्य, और 'Easy' का अर्थ है आसान, जो इस संदर्भ में सही नहीं हैं। 'Impossible'will be used because it means something that cannot be done. The sentence conveys that it is not possible to discuss health and wellness without addressing mental health. This emphasizes the critical role of mental health in the broader context of well-being. Whereas, 'Beneficial' means advantageous, 'Advisable' means recommended, and 'Easy' means simple, none of which fit this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. It’s 1. _________to have a conversation about health and wellness without talking about mental health. In the United States, nearly one in five adults has a mental illness or mental health condition. That’s nearly 52 million people. However, mental health isn’t just about having or not having a mental illness. Our wellbeing is 2. _________ by all aspects of ourlives and that, in turn, affects how we experience life, work and time with our loved ones.Now, the conversation is more 3. _________than ever. In 2021, the prevalent feeling of theyear was languishing. Research found that more than 55% of workers weren’t mentally ill, butthey weren’t thriving either. As the pandemic has brought physical health at the centre stagein an unprecedented way, it’s time 4. _________ look at the other major components of wellbeing. The conversation around mental health awareness has never been as public, asprevalent, or as important. After all, building 5. _________ is a critical ingredient to learninghow to care for your mental health. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: B)'Affected'का use सही है क्योंकि 'affected' का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ से प्रभावित होना। वाक्य कहता है कि हमारी भलाई हमारे जीवन के सभी पहलुओं से प्रभावित होती है, इसलिए यहाँ 'affected' उपयुक्त है। 'Different' का अर्थ है 'भिन्न' या 'अलग,' जो इस संदर्भ में सही नहीं है। 'Effected' का अर्थ है 'लागू करना' या 'प्रभाव डालना,' लेकिन इसे 'impacted' के रूप में नहीं उपयोग किया जाता, इसलिए यह भी सही नहीं है। 'Indifferent' का अर्थ है 'उदासीन,' जो इस संदर्भ में फिट नहीं बैठता। 'Affected'will be used because it means influenced by something. The sentence states that our well-being is influenced by all aspects of our lives, making 'affected' the correct fit. 'Different' means 'distinct' or 'not the same,' which does not fit in this context. 'Effected' means 'brought about' or 'caused,' but it is not used in the sense of 'influenced,' so it is incorrect here. 'Indifferent' means 'unconcerned' or 'apathetic,' which is not relevant in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. It’s 1. _________to have a conversation about health and wellness without talking about mental health. In the United States, nearly one in five adults has a mental illness or mental health condition. That’s nearly 52 million people. However, mental health isn’t just about having or not having a mental illness. Our wellbeing is 2. _________ by all aspects of ourlives and that, in turn, affects how we experience life, work and time with our loved ones.Now, the conversation is more 3. _________than ever. In 2021, the prevalent feeling of theyear was languishing. Research found that more than 55% of workers weren’t mentally ill, butthey weren’t thriving either. As the pandemic has brought physical health at the centre stagein an unprecedented way, it’s time 4. _________ look at the other major components of wellbeing. The conversation around mental health awareness has never been as public, asprevalent, or as important. After all, building 5. _________ is a critical ingredient to learninghow to care for your mental health. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: D)'Critical'का use होगा क्योंकि इसका अर्थ है "महत्वपूर्ण या आवश्यक।" passage में बताया गया है कि अब मानसिक स्वास्थ्य पर चर्चा पहले से कहीं अधिक महत्वपूर्ण हो गई है। यह दर्शाता है कि यह चर्चा अत्यधिक आवश्यक है, इसलिए 'Critical' सही है। जबकि 'Irrelevant' का अर्थ है अप्रासंगिक, जो यहाँ सही नहीं है क्योंकि चर्चा महत्वपूर्ण है। 'Difficult' का अर्थ है कठिन, लेकिन यहाँ चर्चा कठिनाई के बारे में नहीं है बल्कि उसके महत्व के बारे में है। 'Unnecessary' का अर्थ है अनावश्यक, जो passage के भाव को पूरी तरह से गलत तरीके से व्यक्त करता है। 'Critical'will be used because it means "important or necessary." The passage states that the conversation about mental health has become more significant than ever, highlighting its necessity. Thus, 'Critical' fits perfectly. 'Irrelevant' means not related or unimportant, which doesn’t suit the context as the conversation is emphasized as important. 'Difficult' means hard to do or understand, which is not relevant here since the focus is on importance, not difficulty. 'Unnecessary' means not needed, which is completely opposite to the meaning conveyed in the passage.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. It’s 1. _________to have a conversation about health and wellness without talking about mental health. In the United States, nearly one in five adults has a mental illness or mental health condition. That’s nearly 52 million people. However, mental health isn’t just about having or not having a mental illness. Our wellbeing is 2. _________ by all aspects of ourlives and that, in turn, affects how we experience life, work and time with our loved ones.Now, the conversation is more 3. _________than ever. In 2021, the prevalent feeling of theyear was languishing. Research found that more than 55% of workers weren’t mentally ill, butthey weren’t thriving either. As the pandemic has brought physical health at the centre stagein an unprecedented way, it’s time 4. _________ look at the other major components of wellbeing. The conversation around mental health awareness has never been as public, asprevalent, or as important. After all, building 5. _________ is a critical ingredient to learninghow to care for your mental health. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: B)'To'का use होगा क्योंकि "it’s time to" phrase में हमेशा 'to' preposition का उपयोग होता है। यह phrase किसी कार्य को करने के सही समय को दर्शाता है। उदाहरण के लिए, "It’s time to act," या "It’s time to learn." Blank में दिया गया संदर्भ यह बताता है कि अब मानसिक स्वास्थ्य के अन्य पहलुओं पर ध्यान देने का समय है। 'About' का अर्थ होता है किसी विषय के बारे में, जो यहाँ उपयुक्त नहीं है। 'For' का उपयोग आमतौर पर उद्देश्य या किसी व्यक्ति के लिए किया जाता है, जो यहाँ फिट नहीं होता। 'Of' का अर्थ होता है "का", जो इस context में अप्रासंगिक है। To'will be used because the phrase "it’s time to" always pairs with the preposition 'to.' This phrase indicates the right moment for an action. For example, "It’s time to act," or "It’s time to learn." The blank in the passage suggests that it is now the time to focus on other aspects of mental health. 'About' means regarding a subject, which is not suitable here. 'For' is used to denote purpose or benefit, which doesn’t fit in this context. 'Of' implies possession, which is irrelevant here.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. It’s 1. _________to have a conversation about health and wellness without talking about mental health. In the United States, nearly one in five adults has a mental illness or mental health condition. That’s nearly 52 million people. However, mental health isn’t just about having or not having a mental illness. Our wellbeing is 2. _________ by all aspects of ourlives and that, in turn, affects how we experience life, work and time with our loved ones.Now, the conversation is more 3. _________than ever. In 2021, the prevalent feeling of theyear was languishing. Research found that more than 55% of workers weren’t mentally ill, butthey weren’t thriving either. As the pandemic has brought physical health at the centre stagein an unprecedented way, it’s time 4. _________ look at the other major components of wellbeing. The conversation around mental health awareness has never been as public, asprevalent, or as important. After all, building 5. _________ is a critical ingredient to learninghow to care for your mental health. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: C)Awareness'का use होगा क्योंकि "awareness" का अर्थ है जागरूकता, जो मानसिक स्वास्थ्य की देखभाल सीखने के लिए एक महत्वपूर्ण घटक है। Passage में इस बात पर ज़ोर दिया गया है कि मानसिक स्वास्थ्य के प्रति बातचीत और जागरूकता बढ़ाने की आवश्यकता है, इसलिए 'awareness' यहाँ सही उत्तर है। जबकि 'Dreams' का अर्थ है सपने, 'Family' का अर्थ है परिवार, और 'Hospitals' का अर्थ है अस्पताल, जो इस संदर्भ में उपयुक्त नहीं हैं। 'Awareness'will be used because it means understanding or consciousness, which is a critical component in learning how to care for mental health. The passage emphasizes the importance of building awareness around mental health care, making 'awareness' the most fitting choice here. Whereas: 'Dreams' refers to aspirations, which don’t fit the context. 'Family' refers to relatives, which isn’t relevant to the focus on mental health awareness in this passage. 'Hospitals' are physical structures for medical care, which don’t align with the context of building personal understanding.
